title: BME280 Environmental Sensor Module (I²C)
design_question: How to measure ambient temperature, humidity, and barometric pressure simultaneously with a single low-power I²C device?
selected_approach: Bosch BME280 breakout module (Adafruit #2652 or equivalent). I²C address 0x76; SDO tied to GND. Weather-station sampling mode: osrs_T=1, osrs_P=1, osrs_H=1, filter=4, t_standby=1000 ms. Polled at 0.5 Hz. Adafruit BME280 Arduino library handles register reads and compensation formulas.
rationale: BME280 provides all three environmental parameters from one IC, minimising I²C address space usage and wiring complexity. 3.6 µA at 1 Hz sampling suits battery-powered field stations. ±1 hPa pressure accuracy enables altitude estimation to ±8 m.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Two ICs, two I²C addresses, higher cost; SHT41 is more accurate for humidity but unnecessary for outdoor environmental logging.
DHT22 uses single-wire protocol (timing-sensitive), BMP180 is discontinued; combined accuracy and reliability inferior to BME280.

verification_notes: FACT: 'BME280 Combined Pressure, Humidity, and Temperature Sensor' confirms ±1 hPa, ±3 % RH, ±1 °C accuracy and 3.6 µA sleep current.
supporting_fact_titles: BME280 Combined Pressure, Humidity, and Temperature Sensor
